title: Overview of entire project
excerpt:
How is it that Monday.com not only exists, but has existed for 10 years and still there’s no button / view / feature that allows me to look at my project as a whole??? 
I have to look at a tiny portion of the project at …

posts.author: CharlieFacade (Charlie Kickham)
posts.cookedHtml:
How is it that Monday.com not only exists, but has existed for 10 years and still there’s no button / view / feature that allows me to look at my project as a whole???

I have to look at a tiny portion of the project at any one time. Talk about basic essentials!!
Rubbish!

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @CharlieFacade - have you looked into creating a dashboard view or overview board for a high level look at all projects? There are options out there.

Thanks
Mark

posts.author: CharlieFacade (Charlie Kickham)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Mark, thanks for reaching out.

I have the overview but even with the overview in full screen mode I can’t see my whole project. I have to export to excel and then scroll out. Absolutely ridiculous!

posts.author: Hezibo (Hezi Bosmi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @CharlieFacade
I think it depends on the way you build your dashboards. In a case you want to see a lot of information and data without scrolling you may use widget and make their size smaller.

I’ve built some dashboards for business owners and projects managers that were very satisfied and even told they first know their business.
You can reach out for more help and support.

posts.author: CharlieFacade (Charlie Kickham)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Hezibo,
Can you show me an example? This sounds just what I need.
Thanks
Charlie

posts.author: Sotiris (bootstraps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Charlie,

You can vote here for larger widgets

Full size widgets needed in dashboards Feature requests
So it turns out we had an old board template in our account which has a fullscreen timeline view in it. It seems that if you have an old one you can duplicate the template and create new boards with the full screen view, they just don’t let you add a fullscreen timeline view anymore but haven’t taken it away from legacy users who had it. If you really really really want this you can DM me on here and I could clone the template + copy to your account. To do so, you’d have to make me an admin on y…

Best,

posts.author: CharlieFacade (Charlie Kickham)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks! That’s really interesting. But will that allow me to export the full Gantt to pdf? Or just slightly increase what I can see on the screen?

posts.author: Sotiris (bootstraps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Its is for larger widgets, so you can see more of your gantt.
What we do is zooming out in our browser ("command - "on Mac) on a 4k screen.
I that way you can see a much better overview of your gantt or timeline. however if you zoom out to much the widget can’t be enlarged so that it the problem now.
Personally I prefer the timeline over gantt as it is more compact.
hope this helps

posts.author: jescho (Jess Chodor)
posts.cookedHtml: How are more people not bothered by this?? Using a Gantt or Table widget… there is so much white space and no way to compact the info. The dashboards are a pain to use with all the scrolling they require. Is there somewhere else I can upvote this feature request also? How do we get this resolved?
